strengths: Structured research approach illustrated by a table of main/sub-questions paired with diverse sources
Effective use of ATL research skills, evidenced by the evolution of the research plan towards relevant coding and strategy topics
Sophisticated OPVL analysis that critically evaluates source reliability and limitations
Detailed critical thinking in the debugging example, identifying an API data issue and implementing a sustainable fix
Creative thinking demonstrated through hand-drawn GUI sketches exploring multiple layouts with pros and cons
weaknesses: Timeline tasks lack specification of the resources, tools or platforms to be used for each phase, reducing practical feasibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The application of ATL skills is strong, with clear examples of research, critical evaluation and creative problem-solving. Defining the specific tools and platforms for each task in the timeline would enhance the realism and feasibility of the plan.

strengths: Detailed, sequenced action plan with clear dates and milestones across research, creation and testing phases
Clear articulation of multiple learning goals (coding, algorithm development, research) coherently linked to personal interest
Convincing narrative tying childhood Scratch experience to current Python goals, deepening the relevance of the learning objectives
Succinct, user-centred product goal that aligns the intended algorithm and public accessibility with learning outcomes
Well-constructed successâcriteria table outlining clear, measurable tests (e.g. profitability targets, performance benchmarks)
weaknesses: Product success criteria section lacks specific, measurable target values for precise evaluation (e.g. run-time thresholds, user task completion rates)
Testing metrics in the success-criteria table are not fully integrated into the main product criteria, reducing consistency between planning and evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning demonstrates a thorough and coherent approach, with a strong personal connection and a feasible timeline. To elevate this further, the product success criteria should include explicit, measurable targets and ensure that testing metrics are consistently reflected in the main criteria section.

strengths: Nuanced reflection on how coding work enhanced communication skills, showing transferability of learning
Comprehensive evaluation table linking functional results and quantitative trends back to success criteria, with suggestions for indicator expansion
Deep self-awareness in reflecting on the shift to project-oriented learning, highlighting growth in self-management and motivation
Specific evidence of increased confidence in idea realization, demonstrating personal growth
Thoughtful consideration of long-term impact on career choices and DP course planning
Practical materials evaluation by verifying performance across different laptops and including runtime metrics
weaknesses: Quantitative evaluation would benefit from extended backtesting periods and defined profit-loss thresholds for each criterion
Accessibility survey data (69.8% âYesâ) is not translated into explicit usability targets (e.g. 80% user satisfaction)
Trust-level survey responses are not yet incorporated into measurable success criteria for user perception
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection section shows deep, evidence-based evaluation of both the product and personal learning growth. To strengthen rigor, the student should define explicit quantitative thresholds (for backtesting, usability and trust metrics) and align these with success criteria.

annotations.comment: Your action plan is detailed and sequenced across research, creation and testing phases with clear dates and milestonesâan excellent demonstration of feasible project planning.

You clearly articulate multiple learning goalsâcoding, algorithm development and researchâshowing coherence between personal interest and project outcomes. This focus lays a strong foundation for planning your project.
Your narrative linking childhood experiences with Scratch to your Python goals provides convincing personal connections that deepen your learning goalâs relevance.
The product goal is succinct and user-centred, clearly stating the intended algorithm and its public accessibility, which aligns well with your learning objectives.
Success criteria would be stronger if you specified measurable targets (e.g. âalgorithm runs within 2 s per tradeâ or â70 % user task completion within 5 minâ) to allow precise evaluation.
The table of main and sub-questions paired with varied sources shows a structured research approach that directly supports your learning goals.
Consider extending backtesting periods and defining specific profit-loss thresholds tied to each criterion to enhance the rigor of your quantitative evaluation.
You describe how your research plan evolved to focus on relevant coding and strategy topics, illustrating effective use of ATL research skills with clear examples.
Your OPVL analysis critically evaluates source reliability and limitations, demonstrating sophisticated research skill application in resource selection.
Reflecting on how coding informed your communication skills demonstrates a nuanced evaluation of transferable skills gained from this project.
The table evaluating function and quantitative results links observed trends to success criteria and suggests indicator expansionâstrong evidence of product evaluation.
Your success-criteria table outlines clear tests with measurable outcomes (e.g. 70 % profitability, MacBook Pro performance), demonstrating practical planning for evaluation.
The debugging example shows detailed critical thinking: you identify an API data issue and implement a sustainable fix, highlighting effective problem-solving in creation.
Presenting hand-drawn GUI sketches is an excellent example of creative thinking; you explore multiple layouts and pros/cons before coding, enhancing design effectiveness.
Your reflection on shifting to project-oriented learning shows deep self-awareness and evidence of developing self-management and motivation.
You link increased confidence in idea realization to your learning experience, providing specific evidence of personal growthâexcellent reflection on impact.
The discussion of career and DP course choices based on your project shows thoughtful consideration of long-term impact on your learning trajectory.
Integrate these specific testing metrics into your product success criteria section to ensure consistency between planning and evaluation stages.
Your timeline is well sequenced; to strengthen it further, specify the resources, tools or platforms you will use for each task to enhance feasibility.
Use your surveyâs 69.8 % âYesâ accessibility data to set explicit usability targets, such as 80 % user satisfaction, and refine your criteria accordingly.
Translate trust-level responses into a success criterion (e.g. â¥ 70 % of users rating 4â5) to better measure autonomous functioning against real user perceptions.
Verifying performance across different laptops is an effective way to confirm hardware compatibility; including runtime metrics demonstrates thoughtful materials evaluation.
